_meta.author: scorecard-refine (national autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize NC + ND statewide-exec batch: mo-green, rachel-hunt, steve-troxler (NC), kelly-armstrong, drew-wrigley (ND). State tier. Every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; null where no evidence; no party inference.

mo-green.notes_set: NC Superintendent of Public Instruction (D), sworn in Jan 2025 (term to 2029), confirmed in office June 2026. Record is almost entirely K-12 education policy and consistently progressive there: opposes Opportunity Scholarship vouchers and sought a voucher-funding moratorium; opposes the Parents' Bill of Rights (SB 49), arguing it 'marginalizes LGBTQ+ students'; defended DEI/equity practice against the 2025 Trump DOE certification demand; opposed dismantling the U.S. Dept. of Education and emphasized preserving federal education funding. NCAE-endorsed. No cross-party deviation. Life, marriage (beyond schools), economy, elections, justice, immigration, 2A: no evidence (outside an education superintendent's portfolio) -> null, not scored from party.

4.note: Opposes SB 49's limits on gender-identity instruction, arguing the law 'marginalizes LGBTQ+ students' -- documented contrary to opposing LGBTQ+ promotion in schools.

0.note: Opposes private-school vouchers/Opportunity Scholarships; as Superintendent called for a voucher-funding moratorium. Documented contrary to school choice.

1.note: Opposes the Parents' Bill of Rights (SB 49) -- which mandates parental notification/consent -- calling it a 'false narrative.'

2.note: Opposes SB 49's restrictions on gender-identity/sexuality instruction in K-4 -- contrary to opposing SOGI content in K-12.

1.note: Opposed dismantling the U.S. Dept. of Education and emphasized preserving federal education funding/strings -- documented embrace of the federal role, contrary to declining federal strings.

rachel-hunt.notes_set: NC Lieutenant Governor (D), assumed office Jan 2025 (term to 2029), confirmed in office June 2026. Daughter of former Gov. Jim Hunt. As LG she presides over the NC Senate (no vote); her HARD record is her 2023-24 NC Senate tenure (SD-42, Mecklenburg). Consistent progressive, no cross-party deviation. HARD: voted NO on SB 20 12-week abortion ban override (RC #432), NO on SB 41 pistol-permit-repeal override (RC #79), NO on SB 49 Parents' Bill of Rights; sponsored bills codifying abortion access incl. a constitutional amendment (S909); endorsed by EMILY's List, Planned Parenthood Votes!, Reproductive Freedom For All, Lillian's List; opposes school vouchers; supports red-flag laws and Medicaid expansion. Christian liberty, federal overreach, and several immigration/election roll calls: no confirmed evidence -> null.

0.note: Sponsored bills to codify abortion access incl. a state constitutional amendment (S909, 2024); campaigns on 'reproductive freedom' -- documented contrary to affirming life from conception.

1.note: Voted NO on the SB 20 12-week-ban veto override (Senate RC #432, May 16, 2023); active pro-choice author -- opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by EMILY's List, Planned Parenthood Votes! South Atlantic, Reproductive Freedom For All, and Lillian's List -- documented abortion-industry PAC support.

0.note: Opposes school vouchers/Opportunity Scholarships ('bankrupt our public schools').

1.note: Voted NO on SB 49 Parents' Bill of Rights (parental notification/consent).

2.note: Voted NO on SB 49 (which restricts gender-identity instruction in K-4) and sponsored a counter-bill -- contrary to opposing SOGI content.

0.note: Voted NO on the SB 41 veto override (pistol-permit repeal / concealed carry on religious-school property), Senate RC #79, Mar 28, 2023.

1.note: Campaign supports 'common-sense red flag laws' -- documented gun-control position.

steve-troxler.notes_set: NC Commissioner of Agriculture (R), in office since 2005, re-elected Nov 2024 (term to 2029), confirmed in office June 2026. Deliberately pragmatic/non-ideological ag administrator (works with Democratic Gov. Stein; was a Democrat until the late 1990s). Thin on social/cultural issues by design -- life, marriage, family, christian liberty, elections, justice all NO EVIDENCE -> null (not scored from party). HARD anti-ESG: joined 12 GOP ag commissioners opposing the Net-Zero Banking Alliance. TWO cross-party deviations: (1) personally imposed and legally defended a total firearms ban -- including concealed-carry permit holders -- at the NC State Fair (2014, upheld, still in force), over gun-rights groups' objections; (2) pro-immigrant-farm-labor posture, supporting an extended E-Verify timeline for seasonal ag workers. CAUTION: he answered NO iVoterGuide survey questions -- any positions attributed to that source are invalid and were excluded.

4.note: Joined 11 other GOP ag commissioners (Jan 2024) opposing the Net-Zero Banking Alliance's GHG-disclosure pressure on agriculture -- documented anti-ESG action.

3.note: His office, with grower groups, supported EXTENDING the E-Verify compliance timeline for seasonal ag workers and warns against immigration raids -- documented contrary to mandatory E-Verify for all employment.

0.note: As Commissioner, banned all firearms -- including concealed-carry permit holders -- at the NC State Fair (2014), defying a carry-expansion law; upheld in court and still in force. Documented restriction on lawful carry.

kelly-armstrong.notes_set: ND Governor (R), took office Dec 2024 (term to 2028), confirmed governing June 2026. Prior: ND at-large U.S. Rep (2019-24), ND state senator, ND GOP chair. Mainstream-to-strong conservative by ratings (Heritage lifetime 85%, ACU ~82, NRA A+, SBA A). HARD: cosponsored Life at Conception Act; signed ND HB 1181 (biological-sex definitions) and HB 1144 (school bathroom/pronoun/parental-notification on trans); signed HB 1588 (carry/self-defense enhancement); signed historic property-tax relief (HB 1176) + used line-item vetoes for budget discipline; signed HB 1165 tightening mail-ballot deadlines. NOTABLE CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: voted YEA on the Respect for Marriage Act (2022) codifying same-sex marriage (nearly censured by ND GOP); VETOED the library-content/'book ban' bill (SB 2307) as 'legislating morality through censorship'; deep bipartisan criminal-justice-reform record (EQUAL Act with Jeffries, acquitted-conduct & juvenile-justice bills with Durbin). Ambiguous: vetoed the narrow ESA voucher bill (HB 1540) while affirming support for broader school choice -> left null. CBDC/border roll calls unverified -> null.

0.note: Cosponsored the Life at Conception Act (14th-Amendment personhood from conception) and Born-Alive/Pain-Capable acts; SBA Pro-Life 'A.'

4.note: SBA Pro-Life 'A'-rated pro-life champion; no abortion-industry PAC support documented.

0.note: Voted YEA on the Respect for Marriage Act (2022), codifying federal recognition of same-sex marriage; nearly censured by ND GOP -- documented contrary to affirming exclusive man-woman marriage.

1.note: RFMA YEA vote codified same-sex marriage recognition -- contrary to opposing same-sex marriage in law.

2.note: Signed HB 1181 defining gender words as biological sex and HB 1144 (one-gender bathrooms, no compelled pronouns).

4.note: Signed HB 1144 barring compelled preferred-pronoun use and requiring parental notification on a student's transgender identification -- opposes LGBTQ+ promotion in schools.

1.note: Signed HB 1144 requiring schools to notify parents if a child identifies as transgender -- parental notification.

3.note: Vetoed SB 2307 (library-content / minors' sexual-content restrictions), calling it 'a misguided attempt to legislate morality through overreach and censorship' -- contrary to anti-sexualization/library-content protections.

2.note: Signed historic property-tax relief (HB 1176) and used 7 line-item vetoes to protect the budgeting process; proposed ~10% agency cuts to close a 2026 revenue gap -- documented fiscal/deficit discipline.

3.note: Signed HB 1165 (2025) requiring absentee/mail ballots be returned by close of polls on Election Day, ending the postmark-and-receive-later rule -- tightens mail-in voting.

0.note: Signed HB 1588 strengthening self-defense and concealed-carry rights (NRA-ILA praised); NRA-PVF A+.

1.note: NRA-PVF 'A+' rating and endorsement -- HARD evidence of opposing gun-control measures (red-flag/AWB/registries).

drew-wrigley.notes_set: ND Attorney General (R), appointed Feb 2022, elected 2022; current term to Jan 2027. Won the June 9, 2026 GOP primary; on the Nov 2026 general ballot vs Tim Lamb (Dem-NPL). Textbook conservative AG built on HARD legal actions (~48 multistate suits, nearly all challenging the Biden admin). HARD: defended ND's abortion ban as named party and moved to trigger it post-Dobbs; defended ND's ban on minors' gender-affirming care and joined the Title IX gender-identity lawsuit; joined the amicus supporting Trump's birthright-citizenship EO; tough-on-crime/fentanyl prosecutor (mandatory-minimum push, pro-law-enforcement programs); enforces ND's porn age-verification law; co-led the 25-state ATF pistol-brace lawsuit (venued in ND). No ideological cross-party deviation. Two legal-mechanics nuances noted (citizenship-proof opinion; abortion-provider health-data defense) -- not policy stances -> left null. Anti-ESG asset-manager-coalition membership unconfirmed -> null.

0.note: Vigorously defends ND's near-total abortion ban as the named party and praises it as 'important pro-life legislation' -- documented pro-life posture.

1.note: Moved to trigger ND's near-total ban days after Dobbs and pledged appeals to keep it in force -- active advancement of near-abolition (exceptions only for rape/incest/life).

4.note: Sued by abortion providers and a consistent pro-life defender; no abortion-industry PAC support documented.

2.note: Joined the multistate suit against the Biden Title IX rule redefining 'sex' to include gender identity, and defended ND's ban on gender-affirming care for minors (named defendant; ban upheld).

4.note: Challenged Biden's Title IX gender-identity rule and transgender sports guidance in schools -- opposes LGBTQ+ promotion in policy.

3.note: ND's porn age-verification law (HB 1561) is enforced through the AG's office, which receives annual compliance reports -- aligned with age-verification on pornographic content.

0.note: Affirmative pro-law-enforcement record: cleared the sexual-assault-kit backlog, runs the Peace Officer Memorial Service, public transparency on officer cases.

1.note: Champion of mandatory-minimum/tougher sentencing for violent and firearm crimes; publicly opposes leniency -- opposes soft-on-crime.

2.note: Pushed tougher, proportional sentencing for violent/firearm offenders -- proportional punishment of evildoers.

1.note: ~48 multistate suits challenging Biden-administration mandates (EPA coal rule, FHWA CO2 mandate, DACA/ACA rule) -- declining unconstitutional federal strings.

2.note: Resisted federal overreach across guns (ATF pistol-brace suit), life (abortion-ban defense), and education (Title IX).

3.note: Frames his litigation as defending ND sovereignty against a federal government 'hostile to the law' -- affirms state sovereignty.

4.note: Joined NEPA-review challenges and applauded a NEPA win limiting federal control over state land/projects.
Joined the multistate amicus brief supporting Trump's executive order ending birthright citizenship (Feb 2025).
Co-led the 25-state coalition (with WV) challenging the ATF pistol-brace rule, venued in the District of ND alongside the NRA -- opposes ATF overreach.
